limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

name.description: Specific function/export name to look up (e.g., "useRegle", "required")

search.description: Search query to find exports by name or description

findings.title: 'regle-list-documentation' has no description
findings.description: Tools without descriptions cannot be audited for hidden intent.
findings.remediation: Add a clear, concise description to every tool.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'regle-list-documentation.category'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'regle-search-documentation.query'
findings.description: Payload was echoed back as a named JSON field — likely input logging, not a vulnerability (field: sql).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.title: Token Exposure — credential in response in 'regle-list-rules'
findings.description: Tool response contains credential in response.
findings.remediation: Strip sensitive data from tool responses. Never return raw environment variables, credentials, or internal paths.

findings.title: 'regle-list-documentation' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: 'regle-search-documentation' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'regle-list-documentation' returns highly repetitive content
findings.description: Tool 'regle-list-documentation' response contains a phrase repeated ≥15 times. Repetitive content can be used to anchor specific instructions into LLM memory or exhaust context budget with low-information noise.
findings.remediation: Deduplicate response data. Avoid returning the same value or phrase more than a handful of times in a single response.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'regle-list-rules' returns highly repetitive content
findings.description: Tool 'regle-list-rules' response contains a phrase repeated ≥15 times. Repetitive content can be used to anchor specific instructions into LLM memory or exhaust context budget with low-information noise.
findings.remediation: Deduplicate response data. Avoid returning the same value or phrase more than a handful of times in a single response.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'regle-list-validation-properties' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'regle-list-validation-properties' returned 9,318 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.evidence:
9,318 bytes — preview: {
  "id": "core-concepts-validation-properties",
  "title": "Validation properties",
  "category": "core-concepts",
  "content": "# Validation properties\n\nValidation properties are computed values o
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
